zh.lifeSummary: William de Lancaster II是William de Lancaster I和Gundred de Warenne的兒子，出生在英國波藍德。他於1170年之前與Helawise de Stuteville在英國維斯特莫藍德結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於從1184年1月8日到1185年1月7日，埋葬在英國藍卡爾巴羅 在 福內斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: William de Lancaster II是William de Lancaster I和Gundred de Warenne的儿子，出生在英国波藍德。他于1170年之前与Helawise de Stuteville在英国維斯特莫藍德结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。他去世于从1184年1月8日到1185年1月7日，埋葬在英国藍卡爾巴羅 在 福內斯。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Lancaster
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Lancaster in northwestern England, which is recorded as Loncastre in 1086. The place takes its name from the river Lune + Old English ceaster ‘city, Roman fortification’ (from Latin castra ‘legionary camp’). The river name is probably British, perhaps related to Gaelic slán ‘healthy’, ‘salubrious’.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Battle of Hastings
historicEvents.description: Battle of Hastings was fought between the Norman-French army and the battle-wary English army on October 14, 1066. Duke William of Normandy believed he was the rightful king rather than Harold, who had been crowned king several months earlier. Harold was killed in battle and William was crowned king on Christmas Day 1066.

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: William de Lancaster II was born in Cumberland, England as the son of William de Lancaster I and Gundred de Warenne. He married Helawise de Stuteville before 1170, in Westmorland, England. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died from 8 January 1184 to 7 January 1185, and was buried in Barrow in Furness, Lancashire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: William de Lancaster II est né à Cumberland, Angleterre, fils de William de Lancaster I et Gundred de Warenne. Il a épousé Helawise de Stuteville avant 1170, à Westmorland, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé du 8 janvier 1184 au 7 janvier 1185, et a été enterré à Barrow in Furness, Lancashire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: William de Lancaster II wurde in Cumberland, England, als Sohn von William de Lancaster I und Gundred de Warenne geboren. Er hat vor 1170 in Westmorland, England Helawise de Stuteville geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb von 8. Januar 1184 bis 7. Januar 1185 und wurde in Barrow in Furness, Lancashire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: William de Lancaster II è nato il nel Cumberland, England come figlio di William de Lancaster I e Gundred de Warenne. Lui sposato Helawise de Stuteville prima del 1170, nel Westmorland, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì da 8 gennaio 1184 a 7 gennaio 1185, e fu sepolto a Barrow in Furness, Lancashire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: William de Lancaster IIはイギリスクウンバーランドでWilliam de Lancaster IとGundred de Warenneの息子として生まれた。1170年以前にイギリスウエストモーランドでHelawise de Stutevilleと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼はから1184年1月8日まで1185年1月7日に死去し，イギリスランカシュアイアバーロー イン ファーネスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William de Lancaster II(은)는 영국 쿠벌랜드에서 William de Lancaster I(와)과 Gundred de Warenne의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1170년 이전 영국 웨스트모를랜드에서 Helawise de Stuteville(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1184년 1월 8일부터 1185년 1월 7일까지 사망했으며 영국 랭카실르 배로 인 퍼네스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: William de Lancaster II nasceu em Cumberland, Inglaterra filho de William de Lancaster I e Gundred de Warenne. Ele casou-se com Helawise de Stuteville antes 1170, em Westmorland, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu de 8 de janeiro de 1184 a 7 de janeiro de 1185, e foi sepultado em Barrow in Furness, Lancashire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: William de Lancaster II родился в местности кумбирланд, Англия. Его родителей звали William de Lancaster I и Gundred de Warenne. Он женился на женщине по имени Helawise de Stuteville перед 1170 г. в местности вестморланд, Англия. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер с 8 января 1184 по 7 января 1185 и был похоронен в местности барроу ин фьурнесс, ланкашир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: William de Lancaster II nació en Cumberland, Inglaterra como hijo de William de Lancaster I y Gundred de Warenne. Se casó con Helawise de Stuteville antes de 1170, en Westmorland, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió desde 8 de enero de 1184 hasta 7 de enero de 1185, y fue sepultado en Barrow in Furness, Lancashire, Inglaterra.
